text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Eldergrove, there lived a peculiar, old man named Grimoire Gary. He was respected, albeit known by some for his eccentricities. Gary was a bookworm, collecting rare tomes from far and wide, and his cottage became a veritable treasure trove of knowledge.

One crisp autumn morning, as the village awoke to the gentle rustling of leaves, Gary’s bookshelf quaked. From within one particularly rattly tome sprang out a life-like beetle, its wings clicking against the pages. The insect buzzed around Gary, who merely chuckled and offered it a piece of stale bread. The beetle took it with grace, as if it were a gem.

Gary’s day progressed with the usual routine: reading, pondering the arcane, and helping villagers with their quirky questions. Just as he was about to close his weathered folio with a soft thud, a loud clatter echoed through the cottage. Rushing
